evidence_quotes.quote: publishers are currently able to delete a customer's product without any repercussion.

For example, a video game called "The Crew 2" published by the company Ubisoft was recently removed from the online stores of Ubisoft, rendering it unplayable for anyone who had purchased the game, even if it was physically bought.

paragraphs.text: Hello, I am writing to add my own opinion on this matter. Video games and softwares should also be taken in account in the Digital Fairness Act, considering publishers are currently able to delete a customer's product without any repercussion. For example, a video game called "The Crew 2" published by the company Ubisoft was recently removed from the online stores of Ubisoft, rendering it unplayable for anyone who had purchased the game, even if it was physically bought. A company should not be able to take away a customer's product without consequences. This practice should be illegal, and I politely request that you incorporate a way to protect customers from it.
